translation.en: The fire is burning in the forest.

notes_md:
**cháy** = to burn, to be on fire. It’s usually intransitive: something *cháy* by itself (fire, candle, house, food).

For burning something on purpose, use **đốt cháy** (đốt = set on fire). Opposite idea: **tắt** (go out, be extinguished).

It also means “be burnt/overcooked” for food, and very commonly “sold out” in **cháy hàng** (literally “goods burned up” = all gone).
exampleSentencesTranslated: You have to be careful when cooking so you do not burn the food.
I saw the candle on the table still burn when everyone left.
If the wire is damaged, it can easily burn and cause danger.

example.es: El fuego está ardiendo muy grande.
example.en: The fire is burning very large.

definitions.en: To burn; to be on fire.

notes:
cháy = to burn, to be on fire. It’s usually intransitive: something cháy by itself (fire, candle, house, food).

For burning something on purpose, use đốt cháy (đốt = set on fire). Opposite idea: tắt (go out, be extinguished).

It also means “be burnt/overcooked” for food, and very commonly “sold out” in cháy hàng (literally “goods burned up” = all gone).
exampleSentences: Em phải cẩn thận khi nấu ăn để không làm cháy thức ăn.
Anh thấy ngọn nến trên bàn vẫn còn cháy khi mọi người rời đi.
Nếu dây điện bị hỏng, nó có thể dễ dàng cháy và gây nguy hiểm.
